behaviors.description: =SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of values in the referenced range.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is entirely within the SUM function's range-parsing and arithmetic logic. No module boundary needs to be crossed — a single engine object with populated cell values exercises the full path. REAL-INFRA: No I/O or subprocess. LLM-DEP: Deterministic arithmetic. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because the bug (wrong sum) would appear even with a single FormulaEngine instance; no inter-module handoff is required to expose it.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a FormulaEngine, set A1–A10 to known numeric values (including zeros and negatives), evaluate =SUM(A1:A10) in cell B1, and assert the result equals the expected arithmetic sum.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing another formula-bearing cell (=B1+1 where B1=A1*2) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure appears at the handoff between the dependency resolver and the evaluator — specifically, whether B1's formula is fully evaluated before C1's formula consumes it. A unit test of the evaluator with a stubbed dependency resolver hides ordering bugs. REAL-INFRA: No I/O needed; in-process wiring suffices. LLM-DEP: Deterministic. MULTI-STEP: Two evaluation steps, but they are not agentic. REJECTION-GATE: System was rejected because no OS or network boundary is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire A1=3, B1==A1*2, C1==B1+1 into the engine. Trigger evaluation of C1 and assert the result is 7. Then change A1 to 5 and re-evaluate C1, asserting the result is 11, verifying that intermediate dependency values propagate correctly.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1→B1→A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping infinitely.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Cycle detection requires the formula parser to build the dependency graph and the evaluator to consult it during traversal. Mocking either component hides the real failure (infinite recursion or stack overflow during evaluation). The failure only surfaces when graph-building and evaluation are actually wired together. REAL-INFRA: No I/O needed. REJECTION-GATE: Unit was rejected because stubbing the graph builder means you're testing the algorithm in isolation, not whether the engine actually prevents the loop.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1==B1+1 and B1==A1+1 in the engine. Attempt to evaluate A1 and assert that the engine returns a CircularReferenceError (or equivalent sentinel) without hanging or throwing a stack overflow.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP returns the correct value on exact match and an error value when the key is absent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is entirely inside the VLOOKUP function's search and return logic — key comparison, match-type handling, and error generation. No inter-module handoff is required to reproduce wrong-key-returned or missing-error bugs. REAL-INFRA: No I/O. LLM-DEP: Deterministic lookup algorithm. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because both the hit and miss code paths live within a single function boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a 3-column lookup table in the engine (e.g., rows: [1,'A',10],[2,'B',20],[3,'C',30]). Assert =VLOOKUP(2,range,3,0) returns 20. Assert =VLOOKUP(99,range,3,0) returns the #N/A error value.

behaviors.description: Nested IF conditions (=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")) evaluate correctly for values in each branch.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure lives entirely in the IF function's recursive argument evaluation — wrong branch selection or incorrect short-circuit logic. A single engine with one cell input exercises the full code path. REAL-INFRA: None. LLM-DEP: Deterministic. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because no handoff between modules is needed; the entire evaluation occurs within the expression evaluator.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to 5, 15, and 25 in three separate assertions. For each, evaluate =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low") and assert the results are "low", "medium", and "high" respectively.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values and skips empty cells rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The empty-cell skip behavior is a conditional branch inside the CONCATENATE implementation. No cross-module handoff is needed to expose the bug (empty cells being joined as '' instead of being omitted). REAL-INFRA: None. LLM-DEP: Deterministic. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because the behavior is self-contained within the built-in function implementation.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1='Hello', A2='', A3='World' (A2 explicitly empty). Evaluate =CONCATENATE(A1,A2,A3) and assert the result is 'HelloWorld'. Also assert with a known separator variant that the empty cell contributes no characters.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (=Sheet2!A1+1) resolves correctly when that sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure occurs at the handoff between the cross-sheet reference resolver (which must look up the sheet registry and fetch Sheet2's cell model) and the expression evaluator. Mocking the sheet lookup always returns a value, hiding bugs in sheet-name parsing, registry lookup, and the boundary where Sheet2's cell object is handed to Sheet1's evaluator. REAL-INFRA: No filesystem or network needed. REJECTION-GATE: Unit rejected because stubbing the sheet registry hides the actual lookup path; System rejected because no OS boundary is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Create an engine with two sheets. Set Sheet2!A1=10. In Sheet1, set B1==Sheet2!A1+1. Evaluate Sheet1!B1 and assert it returns 11. Also assert that referencing a non-existent sheet returns a #REF! error.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Topological-order correctness is a property of the dependency graph traversal and dirty-flag propagation components working together with the evaluator. A unit test of the graph sorter in isolation doesn't catch bugs where the evaluator pulls stale values because dirty propagation didn't fire in the right order. The real failure only surfaces when the change-notification system, the graph traversal, and the evaluator are wired together. REAL-INFRA: None needed. REJECTION-GATE: Unit rejected for the above reason; System rejected because no OS boundary is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain A1→B1(=A1*2)→C1(=B1+3)→D1(=C1-1). Change A1 from 5 to 10. Assert D1 evaluates to 22 (not a stale value from prior chain), confirming evaluation proceeded in A1→B1→C1→D1 order.

behaviors.description: Undo after a formula edit restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Undo correctness depends on three subsystems working together: the undo stack (which must snapshot both the formula text and the pre-edit computed state), the formula editor (which must push to the stack on edit), and the dependency recalculator (which must re-run after undo). Mocking any one of them hides the real failure — e.g., undo restoring the formula text but not triggering recalculation of dependents. REAL-INFRA: No I/O needed. REJECTION-GATE: Unit rejected because the bug appears only at subsystem boundaries; System rejected because no OS primitive is required.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5 and B1==A1*2 (result: 10). Edit B1's formula to =A1+100 (result: 105). Assert B1==105. Invoke undo. Assert B1's formula is =A1*2 AND B1's evaluated value is 10, confirming both formula text and computed dependent state were fully restored.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and reloading it produces identical cell values and formulas with no data loss or floating-point precision change.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure lives at the serializer/deserializer boundary — precision loss in float encoding, formula string truncation, or cell metadata dropped during round-trip. REAL-INFRA: The custom binary format's round-trip can be exercised with an in-process BytesIO buffer rather than a real file; the failure mode (bit-level encoding errors) is fully reproducible in-process. Using a real file adds no new failure detection for precision or data-loss bugs. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered because the issue mentions disk persistence, but demoted to Integration because an in-memory buffer exercises the same serialization code paths and a real file adds no new failure detection for the precision/data-loss failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with integer cells, float cells (e.g., 1/3 and π to full precision), formula cells (=A1*B1), and empty cells. Serialize it to a BytesIO buffer, deserialize back into a new engine, and assert that every cell's value and formula string are bit-for-bit identical to the original.
